story.text: As the loop, I need a foreign reviewer's response to land as value-gated atoms under one proposed arc, because today the envoy only pushes outward (new through seal) and a returned review strands in read-only docs/sources or as report prose the queue never sees - which is retro 0037 one layer up, the loop running beside the work. The inbound seam (envoy respond) closes the return leg: one finding becomes one proposed atom, grouped so a single confirm-arc queues the whole review, with provenance back to the package and its seal, and a response_received receipt on the disclosure ledger. This v1 corrects one authoring defect in v0: v0 declared incidence.serves: [] and so stranded at owner-stamp.bdo.v1 with no arc to satisfy it. The inbound seam is envoy-reception work, and epic.envoy-reception graduated its pieces into epic.experience-layer (the spawn rail, the model registry, the editor) - so this piece files there, where 'external families [are] the deliberate, receipted exception riding the envoy ledger' is the arc's own glue. bdo confirmed the arc: when asked 'what arc should it?', the answer was epic.experience-layer (adm.6f34c7fe4654, confirmed).

source_artifacts: bdo, chat 2026-06-17: 'what arc should it?' -> epic.experience-layer; epic.envoy-reception absorbed into epic.experience-layer (its pieces 'graduated here')

_note: v1 supersedes atom.inbound-envoy-seam.v0: the only change is incidence.serves [] -> [epic.experience-layer], the arc-filing v0 was authored without (which left it stuck at owner-stamp with no confirmation to satisfy it). The seam work itself (done-line 0057) is unchanged and already on main; this version only gives it the arc it belongs to so the loop can carry it. v0's receipts stand as history; editing-the-atom-mints-a-new-version is the sanctioned amend (identity-is-content-hash). This session re-versioned it, so an independent node judges v1's value line (D-2). Never mutated after writing: the fold over .ai-native/log/ is the live state (D-5).
